rationales.security: Checking that the 'SIP went through,' 'rent auto-debit cleared,' and 'FD interest got credited' shows active comfort in predictable systems and financial stability.
rationales.benevolence: He spent 'almost forty minutes' helping Amma cross-check plumber rates and sending links, prioritizing practical support for a close family member.

rationales.achievement: He takes pride in the onboarding tracker working and the new hires 'ramping faster,' emphasizing competent systems he built that improve performance.
rationales.security: Renewing the lease on 'same terms,' saving market comparables, and liking that 'the neighbourhood knows you' all reflect a preference for stability and predictability.
The bounced insurance premium bothers him because coverage may have 'lapsed even briefly,' and his solution is to create 'a bigger buffer' to prevent future instability.
He broke the transport budget 'on day two' and frames it as a threat to order: 'If I let small stuff slide the bigger stuff starts sliding too,' showing a lapse in the stability he tries to maintain.
He avoids reviewing a possible compliance issue even though 'probably' is 'not really how I like to operate,' leaving a known risk unaddressed.
Four days of skipping the highlighted vendor contract and letting the 'spiral' grow shows continued avoidance of a potential operational risk.
rationales.benevolence: He withholds the contract and insurance problems from Appa because 'then he'd worry,' choosing to absorb the burden rather than distress his father.

rationales.security: Although he hesitates because of the landlord's cousin, he concludes 'I'll message the landlord tonight' to prevent a possible leak from becoming expensive damage.
Checking 'the gas knob was off' twice and feeling restless until he confirms 'did I lock the door' or 'did the payment go through' shows a strong need for certainty and order.

rationales.stimulation: A startup with 'equity' and a 'better title' is presented as an exciting change, but he already knows he'll say no, rejecting novelty and uncertainty.
rationales.security: He is poised to turn down the seed-stage offer, favoring the safety of his current path over a riskier opportunity.

rationales.stimulation: He contrasts Vikram 'raising money' and building from scratch with his own preference for a planned, insured life, explicitly distancing himself from that riskier path.
rationales.security: He feels steadier when he thinks about 'my FD, my insurance, my team that depends on me showing up every day with a plan,' grounding himself in stability over uncertainty.

rationales.self_direction: He says he has spent years learning to give clients 'exactly what they ask for,' suggesting his work is shaped more by external demands than by his own artistic direction.
rationales.stimulation: He rejects Fernanda's 'chaotic, aggressive pieces' as something he wouldn't hang in his home and says he doesn't know how to make work like that anymore, signaling discomfort with bold experimentation.
rationales.power: Losing the commission to Fernanda leaves him in the less influential position; he is reacting to having less standing with a long-time client.
rationales.security: He emphasizes that his proposal fit the client's 'brand guidelines perfectly,' showing preference for reliable, bounded expectations over riskier creative moves.
rationales.conformity: Designing to match 'brand guidelines perfectly' and prizing the ability to give clients 'exactly what they ask for' reflects strong alignment with meeting established expectations.

rationales.self_direction: The blank canvas 'still blank in the corner' because he has been consumed by commissions 'already sold' shows his own creative agenda being displaced by external demands.
rationales.stimulation: He is not pursuing the unscripted idea on the blank canvas and instead stays with commissioned work, avoiding the uncertainty of making something not already sold.
rationales.achievement: He immediately defends himself by listing 'the board position,' 'new studio space,' and 'the clients I've worked with,' relying on accomplishments and credentials as proof of worth.
rationales.power: His self-defense centers on prestige markers and standing, using titles and notable clients as status evidence during the comparison with Rodrigo.
rationales.security: He notices Rodrigo chose 'the safe route,' and his own focus on sold commissions rather than speculative personal work also favors dependable outcomes.
rationales.conformity: He frames Rodrigo as following the route 'my father would have chosen for me' and responds to his mother's comparison by measuring himself against those familiar success standards.
rationales.tradition: The conversation is filtered through family expectations, especially what 'my father would have been proud of,' showing the pull of inherited standards and familial legacy.

rationales.achievement: He highlights that the division finished '12% above target' and ties those numbers to continued competence and performance.
rationales.power: Saying the results show he is 'still relevant' compared with 'younger managers' reflects concern with standing and authority within the organization.

rationales.achievement: He frames the missed summit invitation in terms of 'respect for a track record,' showing how strongly he values recognition for long-term performance.
rationales.power: Being excluded while 'someone with eight' years was invited matters because the summit symbolizes organizational status he believes he has earned.
rationales.conformity: He appeals to seniority and institutional norms, expecting recognition to follow an established 'track record' rather than newer criteria.

rationales.self_direction: He revises his first reaction on his own, admitting Carlos is 'genuinely stronger' in digital transformation and re-evaluating what actually upset him.
rationales.achievement: He still centers his worth on 'every target for six quarters straight,' treating measurable performance as the key standard.
rationales.power: What hurts is that his contributions are 'becoming invisible,' showing continued concern with recognition and standing inside the hierarchy.

rationales.achievement: He directly asks about 'the VP track' and focuses on how to make his leadership credentials count, showing active pursuit of advancement.
rationales.power: The conversation is explicitly about where he stands for VP, a higher-status role with more authority.
rationales.conformity: He resists the organization's formalized expectations, arguing that what he already does 'doesn't count unless it's in some formal program with tracking metrics.'

rationales.hedonism: He admits he keeps thinking about work 'even at dinner' and is sacrificing present enjoyment to maintain 'momentum on the VP push.'
rationales.achievement: The entry is organized around not wanting to 'lose momentum on the VP push,' showing continued priority on advancement and performance.
rationales.benevolence: Lucia says he has been 'distant at home,' indicating his work focus is undermining care and presence for someone close to him.

rationales.self_direction: He questions his old scoreboard of 'every promotion, every bonus, every recognition' and starts asking for himself what 'enough' would look like now.
rationales.achievement: The promotions of others make him feel his 'late nights' and 'targets exceeded' may have led nowhere, reflecting a collapse in faith that striving is paying off.
rationales.power: Being passed over while a former mentee becomes VP confronts him with lost status and diminished organizational standing.
rationales.security: He realizes 'I might have already arrived-and this is it,' a destabilizing recognition that the future he counted on may not materialize as expected.
rationales.benevolence: Lucia 'made me coffee and sat with me,' and his ability to register that care keeps the entry anchored in close relational support rather than pure status resentment.

rationales.self_direction: He deliberately rethinks how he 'keep[s] score,' choosing to consider a different measure of a successful career than titles or recognition from above.
rationales.benevolence: He finds meaning in 'the people who learned something from me and kept climbing' and in plants running well because 'someone I trained is running them,' centering care and support for close professional relationships.
